chapter: Chapter 1: The Story of Village Palampur

questions.question: Which of the following is considered 'Human Capital'?

options.C: Knowledge and enterprise to combine other factors

questions.explanation: Human capital is the knowledge and enterprise required to put together land, labour, and physical capital to produce an output.

questions.question: The main reason why farmers in Palampur are able to grow three different crops in a year is:

options.B: Due to a well-developed system of irrigation
options.C: Due to availability of cheap labor

questions.explanation: Electricity transformed the irrigation system, allowing tubewells to irrigate large areas efficiently, enabling multiple cropping.

questions.question: Which crop is grown by farmers of Palampur during the Rabi season?
questions.explanation: Wheat is the main crop grown during the winter (Rabi) season in Palampur.

questions.question: What is the consequence of the overuse of chemical fertilizers as seen in Punjab?
questions.explanation: Continuous use of chemical fertilizers leads to the degradation of soil health and depletion of groundwater.

questions.question: Out of 450 families in Palampur, how many families cultivate more than 2 hectares of land?
questions.explanation: There are 60 families of medium and large farmers who cultivate more than 2 hectares of land.

questions.question: Why do landless labourers like Dala and Ramkali agree to work for low wages?

options.B: Due to heavy competition for work among labourers
options.C: Because the government sets low wages
options.D: Because they don't need much money

questions.explanation: There is heavy competition for work among farm labourers in Palampur, so people agree to work for lower wages.

questions.question: In modern farming, the farmer needs more _________ than in traditional farming.
questions.explanation: Modern farming requires high-cost inputs like HYV seeds, fertilizers, and pesticides, necessitating more working capital.

questions.question: Marketable surplus is the portion of produce that:

options.B: Is used as seeds for next year
options.C: Is sold in the market by farmers

questions.explanation: Marketable surplus is the grain left over after meeting the family's consumption needs, which is sold for profit.

questions.question: Which non-farm activity uses sugarcane as a raw material in Palampur?
questions.explanation: Mishrilal crushes sugarcane to produce jaggery, which is then sold to traders in Shahpur.

questions.question: How do large farmers like Tejpal Singh use their savings?

options.A: By giving loans to small farmers

questions.explanation: Large farmers use their earnings to provide loans, reinvest in fixed capital, and save for the next season.

questions.question: What percentage of people are engaged in non-farming activities in Palampur?
questions.explanation: While 75% are dependent on farming, only 25% of the people working in Palampur are engaged in non-farm activities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a drawback of the Green Revolution?

options.C: Loss of soil fertility and groundwater depletion

questions.explanation: The intensive use of chemicals and groundwater has led to long-term environmental degradation.

questions.question: Small farmers like Savita face high _________ when borrowing from moneylenders.
questions.explanation: Moneylenders often charge very high interest rates (e.g., 24% for four months) to small farmers.

questions.question: The 'Wheat Revolution' stamp was released in 1968 by:
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi officially recorded the success of the Green Revolution by releasing this special stamp.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of traditional farming?
questions.explanation: HYV seeds are a feature of modern farming, whereas traditional farming uses ordinary seeds.

questions.question: In Palampur, 'Physical Capital' is divided into:
questions.explanation: Physical capital includes fixed capital (tools/buildings) and working capital (money/raw materials).

questions.question: A bigha and guintha are units of:
questions.explanation: Bigha and Guintha are local units for measuring land area in various parts of India.

questions.question: Large farmers use their capital to buy fixed assets like:
questions.explanation: Tractors are fixed capital as they are expensive assets used over many years.

questions.question: What is the status of irrigation for most of the small farmers in India today?
questions.explanation: A large part of the farming in India is still dependent on rainfall, despite the spread of tubewells.

questions.question: Who provides the labour for small farmers?

options.B: The farmers themselves and their families

questions.explanation: Small farmers along with their families cultivate their own fields to save on labor costs.

questions.question: Which factor of production is most abundant in village Palampur?
questions.explanation: Labour is the most abundant factor of production, as many people are landless and seek work.

questions.question: Modern farming requires more industrial goods like _________.
questions.explanation: Modern farming depends on inputs manufactured in industries.

questions.question: Transport services in Palampur connect the village to:
questions.explanation: The road connects Palampur to the big village of Raiganj and the small town of Shahpur.

questions.question: Why is the use of 'Human Capital' vital for production?

options.A: Because it can work for long hours
options.B: Because it is required to coordinate and manage other factors
options.C: Because it is cheaper than machines

questions.explanation: Human capital is the only factor that 'combines' land, labour, and capital to make production possible.

questions.question: The surplus wheat produced by large farmers is bought by:
questions.explanation: Traders buy the surplus wheat at markets and sell it to shopkeepers in towns and cities.
